h2h_summary: Spain have never lost to Uruguay in all recorded meetings, going 2 wins, 2 draws across World Cup and Confederations Cup encounters, with Uruguay yet to claim a victory against La Roja.
wc_history_home: Uruguay are two-time World Cup champions (1930, 1950), including the legendary 2-1 upset of hosts Brazil known as the 'Maracanazo', and have reached the semi-finals four times including a fourth-place finish in 2010.
wc_history_away: Spain won their first and only World Cup title in 2010 in South Africa, defeating the Netherlands 1-0 in extra time, becoming the first European team to win the tournament outside of Europe.
group_context: Group H comprises Spain, Uruguay, Cape Verde, and Saudi Arabia, with Spain as heavy favourites (-450) and Uruguay as the clear second seeds (+400); the final Group H fixture on June 26 — Uruguay vs Spain — is expected to be a decisive match that could determine which team advances as group winner.
strengths_home: Elite centre-back pairing in Araujo and Gimenez providing world-class defensive solidity
Federico Valverde's tireless engine and ability to drive vertical transitions
High-pressing intensity under Bielsa that disrupts opponents and creates quick turnovers
strengths_away: Lamine Yamal's exceptional 1v1 dribbling and creativity on the right flank
Dominant midfield control and ball circulation through Rodri and Pedri
28-game unbeaten run in normal time reflecting squad depth and tactical cohesion
team_style_home: Under Marcelo Bielsa, Uruguay press high and play vertical, transition-focused football with a 4-2-3-1 or 4-3-3 shape, relying on Valverde's engine, set-piece threat, and a resolute defensive block anchored by Araujo and Gimenez.
team_style_away: Spain play a fluid 4-3-3 under Luis de la Fuente featuring elite ball circulation through Rodri and Pedri in midfield, dangerous wide isolation via Yamal and Williams, and high-pressing coordination that has kept them unbeaten in 28 normal-time games since March 2024.
recent_news: Spain is predicted to earn a 1-0 win over Uruguay in the 2026 World Cup. The match is expected to be a tense tactical battle with Spain likely to control the ball and probe for openings.
context: Group H comprises Spain, Uruguay, Cape Verde, and Saudi Arabia, with Spain as heavy favourites (-450) and Uruguay as the clear second seeds (+400); the final Group H fixture on June 26 — Uruguay vs Spain — is expected to be a decisive match that could determine which team advances as group winner.
